metadata.description: Personal Marketplace for development with claude code

plugins.name: tasks
plugins.description: Use the taskmgr file-based task tracker — data model and commands, a skill to turn review findings into well-formed tasks, generic implementer/verifier agents, and a tasks-work workflow that runs ready work through implement, verify, and record.

plugins.name: project-quality
plugins.description: Project-quality toolkit — read-only adversarial reviews (complexity, structure, tests, consistency, docs) runnable individually or as one orchestrated, verified full review, thin human-triggered exec skills (run tests, cut a release, analyze monitoring), and a human-triggered explainer that digests how the project handles a topic from its own docs

plugins.name: grill
plugins.description: Adversarial stress-test on demand — grills any plan, design, change, or decision with pointed questions, each carrying a committed recommended answer and a source, then walks them with you one at a time and ends on a clean / needs-answers gate; read-only and project-agnostic, grounded in a generic critical-engineering value base

plugins.name: github-releases
plugins.description: Language-agnostic GitHub release workflow with quality gates, semver, and release notes

plugins.name: claude-catppuccin
plugins.description: Visual style for Claude Code: Catppuccin color themes (Latte, Frappe, Macchiato, Mocha)

plugins.name: keep-awake-linux
plugins.description: Prevents Linux system sleep while Claude Code is actively working — releases automatically when idle or on session exit

plugins.name: project-explore
plugins.description: Assisted exploratory testing — researches a project, then plays around with the product one action at a time, filing findings as tasks.

plugins.name: html-visualization
plugins.description: Interactive HTML the user opens in a browser, then sends back to Claude — three user-invoked command skills over a shared html-visualize core, each rendering a different surface: question forms (html-visualize-ask), content for inline commenting (html-visualize-feedback), or display-only visualizations (html-visualize-demo); a shared one-shot Node server captures the response and re-invokes Claude
